item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Fable is a version of Mythos the public can access today
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic is releasing Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model available to the public. The model comes with guardrails that block responses in high-risk areas like cybersecurity and biology.

item.title: Rivian starts deliveries of its all-important R2 SUV
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: Founder and CEO RJ Scaringe has called it "maybe the most important thing we've launched to date."

item.title: Apple’s foldable iPhone could be just around the corner
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: The iOS 27 developer beta includes code that references the fold state and screen angle of a device.

item.title: It’s not FAANG anymore. It’s MANGOS.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: With SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI all eyeing massive public debuts, the tech industry may soon have a new class of corporate overlords — and a new acronym to match. Say goodbye to FAANG and hello to MANGOS.

item.title: Apple says it may remove some apps from the App Store if they don’t attract users
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple may begin removing existing apps that it considers stale, low-value, or unable to attract users.

item.title: iOS 27 features we didn’t see onstage
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: While iOS 27 isn't bringing major changes, it's still got many small features that could improve everyday usage for iPhone users.

item.title: Apple brings streaming-style subscription bundles to the App Store
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple is expanding its App Bundles feature to allow developers to partner with one another on discounted subscription packages.

item.title: Apple’s App Store rolls out personalized recommendations
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple will now recommend apps based on your downloads and behavior.

item.title: Sandstone raises $30M to bring AI to in-house legal teams
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Sandstone's Series A was led by Lightspeed Partners, with participation from Sequoia.

item.title: Lovable says it has hit $500M in annualized revenue, with 1 million new projects a week
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Lovable says it has now surpassed $500 million in annualized run-rate revenue and its users are building businesses and replacing internal software.

item.title: How an e-scooter founder raised $5 million to build space data centers
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Orbital founder Euwyn Poon built 250,000 scooters at Spin. Now he wants to launch 10,000 space data centers.

item.title: Evotrex raises $30M to build the RV that doesn’t need a charging station
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The startup is one of many entering the RV space, but it's banking on a hybrid power system that can go far beyond campsites.

item.title: Zepto’s IPO filing reveals fast growth, bigger losses, and a valuation question nobody’s answered yet
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Zepto's advertising revenue jumped 151%, outpacing the company's 104% growth in operating revenue.

item.title: Why Apple’s slow-and-steady AI bet is starting to look pretty smart
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Can Apple's new AI glow-up put to bed accusations that it's losing an all-important industry race?

item.title: Mercor’s Brendan Foody calls out Sequoia, accusing it of ‘dual-pricing’ valuation tricks
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Sequoia is just one of the top firms that sells same equity at two different prices.

item.title: As OpenAI files for IPO, Sam Altman’s eye-scanning company is doing layoffs, report says
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Tools for Humanity, Sam Altman's identity verification company, is reportedly struggling to generate revenue and will downsize its staff.

item.title: Apple’s WWDC AI demos looked more real after $250M false ad settlement
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: The vibe of Apple's 2026 WWDC keynote felt like a spouse proudly listing all the honey-do-list items tackled. One subtle example: the many AI demos of someone standing, phone in hand.

item.title: OpenAI files confidentially for IPO, following Anthropic
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: The filing comes a little more than a week after its main rival, Anthropic, also filed to go public, ramping up the race between the two AI firms.

item.title: Apple plays catch-up at WWDC
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple spent much of its WWDC keynote highlighting fixes, performance improvements, and long-requested features before unveiling its upgraded AI-powered Siri, signaling that the company wants users to see AI as just one part of a broader effort to improve its software.

item.title: Apple bets cheaper AI will woo small developers
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: As AI experimentation grows more expensive, Apple is waiving cloud API costs for developers with fewer than 2 million first-time App Store downloads.
